title: Active & Continuous Learning
concept: Active learning selects only the most uncertain production images for human labeling, making retraining efficient and cost-effective.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the difference between data drift and concept drift?

options.text: Data drift shifts input image statistics; concept drift shifts target definitions
Data drift is for edge devices; concept drift is for servers
Data drift happens during training; concept drift happens after quantization

exercises.explain: Data drift shifts the inputs, while concept drift changes the relationship between inputs and outputs.

cards.title: Don't label everything
cards.body: When your model experiences data drift, you need to retrain it with new images. But hiring humans to label millions of video frames is incredibly expensive. What if the AI could pick out only the confusing images it doesn't understand, and ask you to label only those? This is active learning!

cards.title: Active Learning & Retraining Loops
cards.body: Active learning is a pipeline where the model queries a pool of unlabeled data, identifies samples with low prediction confidence (uncertainty sampling), and flags them for human review. Once labeled, they are fed back into training.

cards.title: A student highlighting hard words
cards.body: If you are learning a language, reading an entire dictionary is a waste of time. Instead, you read a storybook, highlight only the words you don't know, and ask a teacher (human annotator) what they mean. This helps you learn much faster!

cards.title: Autonomous vehicle trigger
cards.body: A self-driving car company logs clips when safety drivers override the vehicle, or when the detector's confidence is between 40% and 60%. These targeted clips are sent to human labelers to build the next training version.

cards.title: More random data isn't the answer
cards.body: You might think labeling more random images is the best way to retrain. In reality, labeling images the model is already 99% confident on adds no new information, wasting labeling budgets on redundant features.

cards.title: Active Learning retraining loop
cards.body: The loop that continuously improves models:

left.text:
Model processes incoming stream
Logs low-confidence flags
Detects data drift indicators
Saves candidate images

right.text:
Humans label flagged candidates
Add new data to training set
Re-run model training script
Deploy updated version

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of active learning in computer vision production?

options.text: To minimize labeling costs by asking humans to label only the most uncertain images
To automatically label all images with 100% accuracy without humans
To compile the model for Apple Neural Engine using CoreML
To add adversarial perturbations to validation datasets

exercises.explain: Active learning optimizes human effort by highlighting only high-value, uncertain samples for manual labeling.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Retraining a model on images where it already makes highly confident, correct predictions is a good use of labeling budget.
exercises.explain: False. Labeling redundant images adds little value to training, wasting resources.

exercises.sentence: Selecting images for human labeling based on how close the model's prediction is to a 50/50 split is called ___ sampling.
exercises.explain: Uncertainty sampling targets data points near the decision boundaries where the model is most confused.

exercises.prompt: Which trigger is best for capturing active learning candidates in a production drone system?

options.text: When the object detector flags a bird with low confidence (e.g. 45%)
Every single frame captured during a 5-hour flight
Only frames where the model is 99.9% confident

exercises.explain: Low confidence indicates the model doesn't recognize the object well, making it a perfect active learning candidate.

exercises.prompt: You run an AI traffic tool. You want to keep improving its accuracy over the next year while spending less than $500 a month on human labelers. What process should you implement?

options.text: Implement an active learning pipeline: flag and store only low-confidence predictions, send them to labelers, and retrain the model monthly.
Send all traffic videos to labelers and run out of budget in week one.
Stop updating the model entirely and accept accuracy degradation.
Randomly choose 10,000 frames every day and delete the rest.

exercises.explain: Active learning using uncertainty sampling is the most cost-effective and modern way to maintain and improve production models on a budget.
